_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
comments: DCWF ksa equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-010 (AI, cosine: 0.664), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.634).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.914 (P=0.967, R=0.867). P exceeds R by 0.100 — the DCWF element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 18 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Knowledge of the AI lifecycle." and "Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift de" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.752 > STS 0.625 (gap +0.127). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, knowledge, lifecycle] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 22.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, lifecycle]. DCWF-only: []. WIDAI-only: [criteria, decommissioning, detection, drift, management, model].
